choices.text: Most REST/tRPC mutation endpoints. A Server Action is an async function with `'use server'` directive that runs on the server but is called from Client Components as if local. Next.js auto-generates the RPC plumbing (serialization, transport, error handling); types flow through end-to-end. Replaces hand-written `/api/posts/create` endpoints + fetch wrappers + duplicated types. Works with native HTML forms via `action={fn}` for progressive enhancement — forms work even when JavaScript fails. Keep REST endpoints for external consumers (mobile, webhooks); use Server Actions for app-internal mutations.

questions.explanation: Server Actions replace most REST mutation endpoints with co-located, type-safe server functions.

questions.prompt: Server Action creates a post, then redirects to /posts list. List still shows old data after redirect. Cause?

choices.text: Missing `revalidatePath('/posts')` (or `revalidateTag('posts')` if you used tags) before the redirect. The /posts page was cached; the redirect navigates there, the cache serves stale data. The fix: call `revalidatePath('/posts')` after the DB write but before `redirect()`. Same pattern for dynamic routes: `revalidatePath('/posts/[slug]')` or use tags on the fetches and invalidate the tag. Forgetting revalidation after a mutation is the most common Server Actions bug.

questions.explanation: Call revalidatePath / revalidateTag before redirect. Otherwise cache serves stale.
